records.name: Pharmacy of Grace
records.what: Nonprofit charitable pharmacy serving uninsured/underinsured patients with affordable medications at nominal cost; sources include donated meds from Dispensary of Hope, government discount programs, and discounted generics. Walk-in pharmacy in Wyandotte County (a second Johnson County/Shawnee location is planned to open July 2026).
records.free_claim: Provides medications at nominal cost to uninsured/underinsured low-income patients; supported by donors and Dispensary of Hope donated medications (per their site and KSHB/JoCo Post coverage).
records.nonprofit_claim: Self-described charitable pharmacy; partners with faith-based Mercy & Truth Healthcare Ministry and Dispensary of Hope charitable network.

records.name: AccessKC (a program of AcruxKC)
records.what: Bi-state Kansas City nonprofit program that issues vouchers covering 100% of the cost of prescriptions (and dental, vision, hearing aids, DME) for low-income uninsured/underinsured residents of the 8-county KC metro. Vouchers redeemed at partner providers.
records.free_claim: Covers 100% of the cost of eligible prescriptions for qualifying applicants (working households up to 200-300% FPL); applicants apply online and receive vouchers.
records.nonprofit_claim: Program of AcruxKC, a 501(c)(3) tax-exempt organization incorporated in Missouri (stated on accesskc.org).

records.name: Dispensary of Hope (charitable medication network)
records.what: National charitable medication distributor that supplies safety-net clinics and charitable pharmacies with free manufacturer-donated medications for uninsured patients. Has dispensing/member sites in Kansas (including Kansas City) and partners with local charitable pharmacies. Use the access-site-finder to locate a participating dispensing site.
records.free_claim: Distributes manufacturer-donated medications free of charge through not-for-profit dispensing sites (charitable pharmacies, FQHCs, safety-net clinics) for uninsured/low-income patients.
records.nonprofit_claim: Charitable medication distributor; network members are all not-for-profit organizations licensed to dispense directly to patients.

records.name: Medicare Extra Help / Part D Low-Income Subsidy (LIS) - via Social Security Administration
records.what: Federal subsidy that helps low-income Medicare beneficiaries pay for Part D prescription drug costs - waives the deductible and premium and caps copays (2026: no more than $12.65 brand / $5.10 generic). Apply through SSA online, by phone, or local office.
records.free_claim: Government program that pays some or all Part D prescription drug costs for qualifying low-income Medicare beneficiaries (Extra Help/LIS).
records.nonprofit_claim: Federal government program administered by the Social Security Administration and CMS.

records.name: SHICK - Senior Health Insurance Counseling for Kansas (Kansas SHIP)
records.what: Kansas's federal State Health Insurance Assistance Program. Free, unbiased counselors screen and enroll low-income Medicare beneficiaries into Part D Extra Help and Medicare Savings Programs and help find affordable prescription medications. Serves KS metro counties (Johnson, Wyandotte, etc.).
records.free_claim: Free counseling that screens and enrolls low-income Medicare beneficiaries into Part D Extra Help and Medicare Savings Programs to reduce/eliminate prescription costs.
records.nonprofit_claim: State/federal SHIP program administered by Kansas Dept. for Aging and Disability Services; services are free.

records.name: Missouri SHIP (formerly CLAIM) - Missouri SHIP
records.what: Missouri's federal State Health Insurance Assistance Program. Free volunteer Medicare counseling that helps beneficiaries enroll in Part D Extra Help, Medicare Savings Programs, and prescription assistance programs to lower drug costs. Serves the Missouri KC metro counties.
records.free_claim: Free counseling to help low-income beneficiaries sign up for programs that pay Part D premiums/prescriptions, including Extra Help and prescription assistance programs.
records.nonprofit_claim: Nonprofit agency, certified federal/state SHIP; does not sell anything or charge for services.

records.name: Missouri Rx Plan (MORx)
records.what: Missouri state pharmaceutical assistance program that coordinates with Medicare Part D to pay 50% of out-of-pocket medication costs for eligible low-income Missourians (those with Medicare plus MO HealthNet benefits). Eligible individuals are automatically enrolled.
records.free_claim: State program pays half of out-of-pocket prescription drug costs for eligible low-income Medicare/MO HealthNet beneficiaries; currently active (FAQ revised Oct 2024).
records.nonprofit_claim: Missouri state government pharmaceutical assistance program administered by MO Dept. of Social Services / MO HealthNet.

records.name: NeedyMeds
records.what: National 501(c)(3) nonprofit directory of patient assistance programs, free/low-cost clinics, charitable pharmacies, government programs, and a drug discount card. Free single point of access to locate medication assistance for KC metro residents.
records.free_claim: Free resource that helps people find manufacturer Patient Assistance Programs, free/low-cost clinics, and government programs to obtain medications at no/low cost; no charge to use.
records.nonprofit_claim: NeedyMeds, Inc. is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization.

records.name: RxAssist
records.what: National directory of manufacturer Patient Assistance Programs (PAPs) with up-to-date information on accessing free/low-cost medications from nearly 100 companies and 700+ medications. Free tool to help KC metro patients/providers locate PAPs.
records.free_claim: Free, comprehensive directory of Patient Assistance Programs to help patients obtain free or low-cost medications.
records.nonprofit_claim: Nonprofit-operated patient assistance program directory (no charge to use information).

records.name: Medicine Assistance Tool (MAT) - PhRMA
records.what: Free search engine for manufacturer Patient Assistance Programs (PAPs) and the prescriptions they cover, helping uninsured/underinsured patients find programs that provide medicines at no or low cost. National; usable by KC metro residents.
records.free_claim: Free tool that connects patients to manufacturer PAPs providing medicines at no cost or very low cost.
records.nonprofit_claim: Operated by PhRMA (Pharmaceutical Research and Manufacturers of America) as a free public resource directing patients to nonprofit/manufacturer assistance programs.

records.name: 211 (United Way) Prescription Assistance Referral - Kansas City metro
records.what: Free, confidential 24/7 referral line connecting KC metro residents to local prescription assistance programs, charitable pharmacies, and free clinics. Dial 211 from any phone.
records.free_claim: Free referral service that connects low-income residents to local prescription assistance programs and charitable medication resources.
records.nonprofit_claim: United Way 211 nonprofit information and referral service.

records.name: Dellwood Community Pharmacy by Rx Outreach
records.free_claim: Walk-in community pharmacy operated by Rx Outreach, a nonprofit pharmacy, to help uninsured people and those struggling to afford medications access affordable/free meds.
records.nonprofit_claim: Operated by Rx Outreach, described as the nation's largest nonprofit, fully licensed pharmacy (501(c)(3)).
records.what: Walk-in charitable nonprofit community pharmacy serving uninsured and low-income patients; co-located with ARCA at the R&R Marketplace.

records.name: Rx Outreach (nonprofit mail-order pharmacy)
records.free_claim: Nonprofit mail-order pharmacy providing low-cost medications to uninsured and underinsured patients nationally; works with patients with or without insurance.
records.nonprofit_claim: Described as the nation's largest nonprofit, fully licensed pharmacy; based in St. Louis, MO.
records.what: By-application nonprofit mail-order pharmacy offering affordable generic and brand medications to low-income/uninsured patients.

records.name: Rx Outreach Fill the Gap Patient Assistance Program
records.free_claim: Provides a formulary of FREE medications (cardiovascular, diabetes, asthma/allergy; 300+ medication strengths) to eligible patients age 55+ at or below 300% of the Federal Poverty Level.
records.nonprofit_claim: Run by Rx Outreach, the nation's largest nonprofit, fully licensed pharmacy.
records.what: By-application free-medication program for low-income residents (age 55+) of the Greater St. Louis region, including St. Louis City, St. Louis County, St. Charles, Jefferson, and Franklin counties.

records.name: Dispensary of Hope
records.free_claim: Charitable medication distributor that delivers donated manufacturer medicine free of cost to people who need it but cannot afford it, via a network of nonprofit safety-net pharmacies and clinics; use the dispensing site finder to locate a participating site.
records.nonprofit_claim: Charitable not-for-profit medication distributor supplying not-for-profit pharmacies, FQHCs, and safety-net clinics.
records.what: National charitable medication distribution network; patients access free meds through participating nonprofit/safety-net dispensing sites (find via site finder).

records.name: NeedyMeds
records.free_claim: Free national service to find Patient Assistance Programs (PAPs), free/low-cost clinics, and disease-based assistance; information available anonymously and free of charge.
records.nonprofit_claim: Registered 501(c)(3) national nonprofit funded by donations.
records.what: National nonprofit search tool to locate free/low-cost prescription assistance programs by medication name or condition.

records.name: RxAssist
records.free_claim: Free comprehensive database of pharmaceutical manufacturer patient assistance programs (free/low-cost meds) plus tools for patients and providers; no charge to use.
records.nonprofit_claim: Nonprofit-oriented public resource for locating manufacturer PAPs; no charge for the information.
records.what: National database of manufacturer Patient Assistance Programs that provide free or low-cost medications to eligible low-income patients.

records.name: Medicine Assistance Tool (MAT)
records.free_claim: Free search engine to find biopharmaceutical industry patient assistance programs that help patients afford or obtain medications at no/low cost.
records.nonprofit_claim: Operated by PhRMA as a free public resource directing patients to manufacturer assistance programs (no charge to use).
records.what: National PAP search engine for patients, caregivers, and providers to locate manufacturer prescription assistance resources.

records.name: Medicare Extra Help / Low-Income Subsidy (LIS)
records.free_claim: Government program that helps low-income Medicare beneficiaries pay Part D prescription drug costs (premiums, deductibles, copays); in 2026 copays capped at $12.65 brand / $5.10 generic, with no copays after the catastrophic threshold.
records.nonprofit_claim: Federal government program administered by the Social Security Administration (SSA) and CMS.
records.what: By-application federal subsidy reducing prescription drug costs for Medicare beneficiaries with income up to 150% of the Federal Poverty Level; apply via SSA (online, phone, or local office).

records.name: MedZou Community Health Clinic
records.what: Free student-run community health clinic (University of Missouri School of Medicine) offering free primary care plus free dispensing of certain medications and a Patient Assistance Program that helps uninsured patients obtain expensive name-brand medications for free, with free pharmacy consultation.
records.address: MU Family Impact Center, 105 E Ash Street, Suite 200
records.free_claim: Site states it provides 'free primary health care' and a 'Patient Assistance Program that helps patients obtain expensive name-brand medications for free' plus 'free selection of certain medications' and 'free pharmacy consultation on prescription medications.'
records.nonprofit_claim: University of Missouri School of Medicine faculty-sponsored, student-run free community clinic (nonprofit/public university program).

records.name: Hannibal Free Clinic
records.what: Free clinic for uninsured low-income adults (18-65, income below 150% FPL) that helps patients obtain medications through pharmaceutical-company patient assistance programs; over $22M in medication support accessed for patients since 2007. By scheduled appointment.
records.free_claim: Clinic provides free non-emergency primary care to uninsured low-income adults; 'over $22 million in medication support has been accessed for patients' and 'clinic volunteers can help you obtain medications from pharmaceutical company patient assistance programs.'
records.nonprofit_claim: Nonprofit volunteer-staffed free clinic (listed in MO state employee charitable campaign and on the National Association of Free & Charitable Clinics ecosystem); not for-profit.

records.name: Northeast Missouri Health Council - Prescription Assistance / Patient Drug Assistance Program
records.what: Nonprofit Federally Qualified Health Center with a dedicated Prescription Assistance Case Manager who helps patients research and enroll in manufacturer Patient Assistance Programs and find affordable medication options; also runs a Patient Drug Assistance Program.
records.free_claim: FQHC provides case-management help (Prescription Assistance Case Manager Shelly Davis, LPN) including 'Patient Assistance Programs research' and a separate Patient Drug Assistance Program to help low-income patients get medications; sliding-scale FQHC services. Note: page also lists for-profit discount cards (GoodRx/WellRx) which do NOT qualify on their own.
records.nonprofit_claim: Nonprofit Federally Qualified Health Center (HHS-funded, Federal Public Health Service deemed status).

records.name: Love Columbia - Prescription Medication Assistance (information & navigation)
records.what: 501(c)(3) nonprofit community resource hub that helps Boone County residents navigate to prescription-assistance resources, including local Columbia/Boone County Public Health prescription aid (up to $350/year), manufacturer PAP tools (RxAssist, Medicine Assistance Tool), Rx Outreach, and MoRx.
records.free_claim: Nonprofit connects residents to free/charitable Rx help, including Columbia/Boone County Public Health prescription assistance (up to $350/year) and manufacturer PAP tools (RxAssist, Medicine Assistance Tool). Note: list also includes the for-profit Missouri Drug Card discount card, which does NOT qualify on its own.
records.nonprofit_claim: 501(c)(3) tax-exempt nonprofit (EIN 20-8801850).

records.name: Rx Outreach
records.what: Nonprofit (501(c)(3)) mail-order charitable pharmacy based in Missouri providing low-cost generic and select brand medications to qualifying low-income patients nationwide with free delivery, no insurance or membership required; income-based eligibility.
records.free_claim: Nonprofit pharmacy provides 'access to affordable generic medications' to income-qualified patients with free delivery, 'No hidden fees. No Memberships'; charitable Fill the Gap program provides limited free medications to eligible uninsured patients.
records.nonprofit_claim: Nonprofit charitable pharmacy (GuideStar/IRS EIN 35-2378788), Missouri-headquartered.

records.name: Missouri Rx Plan (MoRx)
records.what: Missouri's State Pharmacy Assistance Program (SPAP) that helps low-income Medicare Part D enrollees pay for prescriptions by covering 50% of out-of-pocket drug costs; auto-enrolls Medicare + MO HealthNet dual eligibles, plus income-based independent eligibility.
records.free_claim: State pharmacy assistance program that 'pays for 50% of your out-of-pocket costs on medications covered by your Medicare Part D plan' for eligible low-income Missouri residents.
records.nonprofit_claim: Government program administered by Missouri Department of Social Services / MO HealthNet.

records.name: Medicare Extra Help / Part D Low-Income Subsidy (LIS) - Social Security Administration
records.what: Federal program helping people with limited income/resources pay Medicare Part D premiums, deductibles, and copays; estimated average value ~$5,700/yr. Apply through SSA (form SSA-1020); some Medicaid/SSI recipients auto-qualify.
records.free_claim: Federal Low-Income Subsidy helps qualifying low-income Medicare beneficiaries pay Part D drug-coverage premiums, deductibles, and copays (no more than $5.10 generic / $12.65 brand per covered drug in 2026).
records.nonprofit_claim: Federal government program administered by the Social Security Administration.

records.name: Missouri SHIP (formerly CLAIM) - State Health Insurance Assistance Program
records.what: Official Missouri SHIP providing free, unbiased Medicare counseling, including help determining eligibility for and applying to Medicare Extra Help (LIS), Medicare Savings Programs, and prescription assistance programs. Volunteer counselors statewide.
records.free_claim: Nonprofit, federally/state-certified program providing free Medicare counseling that 'helps Missourians on a limited income determine whether they qualify for a Low-Income Subsidy and/or Medicare Savings Programs to offset the cost of their prescription drugs'; does not sell anything or charge for services.
records.nonprofit_claim: Nonprofit agency, official State Health Insurance Assistance Program certified by federal and state government.

records.name: NeedyMeds
records.what: National 501(c)(3) nonprofit providing free information on prescription patient assistance programs (PAPs), manufacturer programs, free/low-cost clinics, and government drug-cost programs via website and free helpline.
records.free_claim: National nonprofit offering free, anonymous information on patient assistance programs and free/low-cost clinics; 'all assistance provided is free of charge.'
records.nonprofit_claim: Registered 501(c)(3) national nonprofit (EIN 46-3091990).

records.name: Medicine Assistance Tool (MAT) - PhRMA
records.what: Free national search engine listing ~900 public and private patient-assistance programs (including manufacturer PAPs offering free meds) to help low-income/uninsured patients access prescriptions; searchable by medication, income, and location. (Transitioning to AmericasMedicines.com in 2026.)
records.free_claim: Free, confidential search engine connecting patients with financial need to ~900 patient assistance programs that help them get their prescriptions.
records.nonprofit_claim: Operated by PhRMA (Pharmaceutical Research and Manufacturers of America), a nonprofit trade association; tool is free to use and indexes charitable/manufacturer assistance programs.

records.name: RxAssist
records.what: National directory of manufacturer Patient Assistance Programs (PAPs) that provide free or low-cost medications to low-income, uninsured patients; free online database referenced by Love Columbia's resource list.
records.free_claim: Free directory of manufacturer patient assistance programs that connect low-income, uninsured patients to free/low-cost medications (cited as a 'pharmaceutical assistance tool connecting patients to manufacturer programs' by nonprofit Love Columbia).
records.nonprofit_claim: Nonprofit/charitable patient-assistance directory (no fee to patients); indexes manufacturer PAPs rather than for-profit discount cards.

records.name: My Neighbor's Charitable Pharmacy
records.free_claim: Missouri's charitable pharmacy serving uninsured low-income patients in Stone and Taney counties; uninsured members receive generic medications at no additional cost beyond a $10/month membership, plus patient-assistance help for brand-name drugs and diabetes supplies. Has dispensed roughly $6.5 million in free prescriptions since 2023.
records.nonprofit_claim: Charitable pharmacy funded through grants and charitable donations; described as Missouri's only charitable pharmacy and a partner in the Dispensary of Hope donated-medication network.
records.what: Walk-in charitable pharmacy providing generic medications and brand-name patient assistance to uninsured, income-qualified residents (single under $46,950; family of four under $96,450).

records.name: Community Clinic of Southwest Missouri
records.free_claim: Free clinic for uninsured, low-to-moderate-income individuals; provides medication within its formulary and prescription-assistance-program enrollment, plus a social worker for prescription-assistance advocacy. Services are free (a $10 donation is requested but not required).
records.nonprofit_claim: Nonprofit; 100% funded by donations, grants, small businesses and fundraisers.
records.what: Walk-in free clinic dispensing in-formulary medications and enrolling uninsured patients in prescription assistance programs.

records.name: Community Medication Access Program (CMAP) - Jordan Valley Community Health Center
records.free_claim: CMAP provides some free medications to uninsured, income-qualified patients age 18+ in Greene County. A collaboration of Mercy, CoxHealth, Jordan Valley and The Kitchen Clinic that has helped more than 4,000 patients obtain an estimated $24 million in medications at no cost. Jordan Valley also runs a Patient Assistance Program (PAP) offering free medications and copay-saving cards.
records.nonprofit_claim: Jordan Valley Community Health Center is a nonprofit Federally Qualified Health Center (FQHC) receiving HHS funding; CMAP is a charitable collaboration of area nonprofit/charity health systems.
records.what: By-application charitable prescription-assistance program for uninsured, income-eligible adults in Greene County (Springfield area).

records.name: Dispensary of Hope (donated-medication network)
records.free_claim: Charitable medication distributor that supplies safety-net clinics and charitable pharmacies with medications donated by pharmaceutical manufacturers, enabling them to dispense free medications to low-income, uninsured patients. Patients access it through local network dispensing sites (use the site finder for Ozarks/Missouri locations such as My Neighbor's Charitable Pharmacy).
records.nonprofit_claim: Not-for-profit charitable medication distributor; its dispensing-site network consists of not-for-profit pharmacies, FQHCs and safety-net clinics.
records.what: National charitable network channeling manufacturer-donated medications to nonprofit dispensing sites; use the site finder to locate Ozarks-area pharmacies/clinics.

records.name: NeedyMeds
records.free_claim: National nonprofit providing free information on over 9,000 patient/medication assistance programs (including manufacturer PAPs offering free or discounted medicines), 15,000 free/low-cost/sliding-scale clinics, and drug discount coupons. Free toll-free helpline at 1-800-503-6897.
records.nonprofit_claim: National 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization; all information provided free of charge.
records.what: Searchable national directory connecting low-income patients to manufacturer PAPs, free clinics, and Rx-assistance programs by drug name or ZIP code.

records.name: Medicine Assistance Tool (MAT)
records.free_claim: Free, confidential search engine from PhRMA giving a single point of access to more than 900 public and private patient-assistance programs, including manufacturer programs that provide free or low-cost medicines to uninsured and underinsured patients. (Transitioning to AmericasMedicines.com in 2026.)
records.nonprofit_claim: Operated by PhRMA (trade association) as a free public-benefit search tool, not a for-profit discount card; it indexes manufacturer/charitable patient-assistance programs.
records.what: National search tool to find manufacturer Patient Assistance Programs and other Rx-assistance resources based on medications, age, income and insurance.

records.name: Medicare Part D Extra Help / Low-Income Subsidy (LIS) - Social Security Administration
records.free_claim: Federal program that pays some or most of Medicare Part D prescription drug costs (premiums, deductibles and copayments) for people with limited income (up to 150% of the federal poverty level). Application is free through SSA; free help applying is available through the State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP).
records.nonprofit_claim: Government program administered by the Social Security Administration and CMS (government help paying for drugs).
records.what: Government low-income subsidy that lowers prescription drug costs for income-eligible Medicare beneficiaries; apply via SSA.

records.name: Missouri SHIP / CLAIM (State Health Insurance Assistance Program)
records.free_claim: Missouri's SHIP (CLAIM) offers free Medicare counseling and education, including free one-on-one help applying for Medicare Part D Extra Help / Low-Income Subsidy and finding prescription-drug assistance. Counselors do not sell anything or ask for money.
records.nonprofit_claim: Government/nonprofit-administered free Medicare counseling program (no sales, no fees).
records.what: Free statewide Medicare counseling that helps low-income Missourians apply for Extra Help/LIS and locate Rx-assistance programs.

records.name: Dispensary of Hope charitable pharmacy (Manhattan, KS) — Ascension Via Christi / MHK Community Health
records.what: Charitable pharmacy filling chronic, non-controlled prescriptions at no cost for uninsured low-income patients across Northeast Kansas. Medications donated by manufacturers via the Dispensary of Hope network; location, staff and supplies provided by Ascension Via Christi, operating fees funded by Manhattan Community Health Foundation. Hours Mon-Thu 1-4 PM.
records.free_claim: Provides cost-free / no-cost prescription medications; eligibility income at or below 300% FPL and no prescription insurance.
records.nonprofit_claim: Operated through nonprofit Ascension Via Christi and the Manhattan Community Health Foundation; medications supplied by Dispensary of Hope, a charitable medication distributor (nonprofit network).

records.name: Health Care Access Clinic (Lawrence, KS)
records.what: Nonprofit free clinic serving uninsured/low-income Douglas County residents; provides over $1 million in free medication annually plus donated diabetes testing supplies, alongside free medical care and referrals to volunteer physicians.
records.free_claim: Provides over $1 million in free medication annually to uninsured/low-income patients.
records.nonprofit_claim: Stated to be a nonprofit organization (free and charitable clinic established 2005).

records.name: River City Pharmacy at Heartland Community Health Center (Lawrence, KS)
records.what: Nonprofit, full-service community-health-center pharmacy using 340B pricing and assistance programs to help underserved/uninsured patients afford medications; patients saved $6.7 million in 2024.
records.free_claim: Nonprofit pharmacy with programs and discounts plus 340B pricing to make medications affordable for underserved patients; $6.7M in patient savings in 2024.
records.nonprofit_claim: Described as a local nonprofit pharmacy, located at Heartland Community Health Center (FQHC).

records.name: Salina Family Healthcare Center Pharmacy (Salina, KS)
records.what: FQHC pharmacy offering medication assistance: free medications through Direct Relief and Americares for qualifying health-center patients, a Manufacturer Assistance Program (MAP) to obtain meds directly from manufacturers at no charge, and $4/30-day, $10/90-day generics. Also a Dispensary of Hope dispensing site in Salina.
records.free_claim: Free medications via Direct Relief and Americares for qualifying patients; Manufacturer Assistance Program obtains medications at no charge; low-cost $4/$10 generics.
records.nonprofit_claim: Nonprofit Federally Qualified Health Center pharmacy; part of the charitable Dispensary of Hope network in Salina.

records.name: Stuber Health Center (St. Joseph, MO)
records.what: Free and charitable clinic providing free/low-cost medical, dental, and women's health care to uninsured/underinsured Buchanan County residents (call to confirm specific medication assistance/referrals).
records.free_claim: Free and low-cost care for uninsured/underinsured; longstanding free & charitable clinic (medication assistance not detailed online — confirm directly).
records.nonprofit_claim: Describes itself as a not-for-profit healthcare clinic and free & charitable clinic.

records.name: Dispensary of Hope (national charitable medication distributor)
records.what: Charitable medication distributor that supplies free, manufacturer-donated medications to nonprofit dispensing sites (charitable pharmacies, FQHCs, safety-net clinics) — including multiple sites across Kansas (Salina, Manhattan, Wichita, Pittsburg, Kansas City).
records.free_claim: Delivers critical medicine free of cost to people who cannot afford it, through nonprofit dispensing sites.
records.nonprofit_claim: Charitable medication distributor working only with not-for-profit licensed dispensing sites; medications donated by manufacturers.

records.name: Rx Outreach (nonprofit mail-order pharmacy / PAP)
records.what: Nation's largest nonprofit, fully licensed mail-order pharmacy and Patient Assistance Program; over 1,000 medication strengths covering most chronic diseases, free delivery in all 50 states, no membership/hidden fees. Serves people at or below 400% FPL regardless of insurance or citizenship status.
records.free_claim: Low-cost prescriptions with free delivery for those at/below 400% FPL; nonprofit Patient Assistance Program, no membership or hidden fees.
records.nonprofit_claim: Nonprofit (501c3) fully licensed mail-order pharmacy headquartered in Maryland Heights, MO.

records.name: NeedyMeds
records.what: Nonprofit clearinghouse listing manufacturer Patient Assistance Programs (PAPs), free/low-cost clinics, government programs, and a drug discount card. Free directory to find help paying for prescriptions; available nationwide.
records.free_claim: Free directory connecting low-income patients to manufacturer PAPs (free meds), free/low-cost clinics, and government assistance programs.
records.nonprofit_claim: 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization funded by donations.

records.name: RxAssist
records.what: Nonprofit-run, comprehensive directory of manufacturer Patient Assistance Programs (nearly 100 companies, 700+ medications) helping patients obtain free or low-cost medications. Nationwide, no charge to use.
records.free_claim: Free, comprehensive directory of Patient Assistance Programs to help patients get free or low-cost medications directly from manufacturers.
records.nonprofit_claim: Charitable PAP information service; free public resource (no program fees).

records.name: Medicine Assistance Tool (MAT) — PhRMA
records.what: Free national search engine for manufacturer Patient Assistance Programs (PAPs) and the prescriptions they cover, helping low-income/uninsured patients find free or reduced-cost medications.
records.free_claim: Free search engine connecting patients to manufacturer PAPs that provide medicines at no or low cost.
records.nonprofit_claim: Free public tool operated by industry association PhRMA to direct patients to charitable manufacturer assistance programs (no fee to user).

records.name: SHICK — Senior Health Insurance Counseling for Kansas (Extra Help / LIS)
records.what: Kansas's federally designated SHIP, run by the Kansas Dept. for Aging and Disability Services. Free, unbiased counseling; screens and enrolls low-income Medicare beneficiaries into Part D Extra Help (Low-Income Subsidy) and Medicare Savings Programs to help pay for prescription drugs.
records.free_claim: Free counseling that screens/enrolls low-income Medicare beneficiaries into Part D Extra Help (LIS) and Medicare Savings Programs (government help paying for drugs).
records.nonprofit_claim: Government program operated by the Kansas Department for Aging and Disability Services.

records.name: Missouri SHIP / CLAIM (Extra Help / LIS counseling)
records.what: Missouri's federally certified State Health Insurance Assistance Program. Free, unbiased, confidential Medicare counseling including help checking for savings and enrolling low-income beneficiaries in Medicare savings/Part D Extra Help (LIS) for prescription drug costs.
records.free_claim: Free Medicare counseling that helps low-income beneficiaries find savings and enroll in Medicare savings programs / Part D Extra Help (LIS) for drug costs.
records.nonprofit_claim: Government-certified State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP).

records.name: Medicare Extra Help / Low-Income Subsidy (LIS) — Social Security Administration
records.what: Federal program that helps people with limited income and resources pay Medicare Part D prescription drug premiums, deductibles, and copayments. Apply via SSA (online, phone, or local office).
records.free_claim: Federal subsidy that pays much of Medicare prescription drug plan premiums, deductibles, and copays for low-income beneficiaries.
records.nonprofit_claim: Federal government program administered by the Social Security Administration / CMS.

records.name: Cairn Health Charitable Pharmacy
records.free_claim: Nonprofit charitable pharmacy serving uninsured Sedgwick County residents without Part D/Rx coverage; income-based eligibility (net monthly income under ~$2,460). Example: 90-day supply of eight medications for $10.
records.nonprofit_claim: Nonprofit charitable organization serving Sedgwick County, KS.
records.what: Walk-in charitable pharmacy providing affordable/low-cost medications and diabetic supplies to low-income uninsured residents.

records.name: Guadalupe Clinic
records.free_claim: Free safety-net clinic for working poor (uninsured adults within 200% of federal poverty level); all medications supplied to patients are free through community and pharmaceutical company partnerships / medication assistance programs.
records.nonprofit_claim: Nonprofit free health clinic operating through community donations, grants, and fundraisers; serving Wichita since 1985.
records.what: Free clinic with on-site dispensing and medication assistance providing no-cost medications to uninsured low-income patients.

records.name: Project Access (Central Plains Health Care Partnership)
records.free_claim: Coordinates donated medical care including prescription medication at no cost to enrolled uninsured low-income Sedgwick County patients; staff help patients obtain donated medication through pharmaceutical companies. Requires referral from a participating clinic (HealthCore, GraceMed, Guadalupe, Hunter Health).
records.nonprofit_claim: Central Plains Health Care Partnership is a 501(c)3 organization.
records.what: By-referral nonprofit program providing free/very-low-cost prescription medication and PAP enrollment help for uninsured low-income residents.

records.name: PrairieStar Health Center - Prescription Programs (340B & PAP)
records.free_claim: FQHC offering a 340B discount drug program for uninsured patients and a Patient Assistance Program connecting patients to manufacturer PAPs for chronic-disease medications (income/insurance criteria; up to 90-day supply for a per-drug processing fee). Serves all regardless of ability to pay.
records.nonprofit_claim: Nonprofit Federally Qualified Health Center (FQHC).
records.what: FQHC prescription-assistance programs (340B discount drugs + manufacturer PAP enrollment) for low-income/uninsured patients in Hutchinson and central Kansas.

records.name: First Care Clinic
records.free_claim: Nonprofit FQHC serving patients regardless of ability to pay; sliding-fee scale. Dedicated prescription-assistance staff access pharmaceutical programs to help uninsured patients obtain medications; on-site 340B pharmacy provides medications at significantly lower prices.
records.nonprofit_claim: 501(c)3 nonprofit Federally Qualified Health Center.
records.what: Free/sliding-scale clinic with on-site 340B pharmacy and prescription-assistance program for uninsured low-income patients in northwest/central Kansas.

records.name: Genesis Family Health (formerly United Methodist Mexican-American Ministries)
records.free_claim: Nonprofit FQHC network serving largely indigent/uninsured population across western Kansas; on-site pharmacy and lab plus linkage to prescription-assistance resources. Treats and serves the uninsured.
records.nonprofit_claim: Nonprofit corporation (501(c)3); serving western Kansas since 1974.
records.what: Community health center network (Garden City, Dodge City, Ulysses, Liberal) with on-site pharmacy and prescription-assistance linkage for uninsured low-income patients.

records.name: Dispensary of Hope (charitable medication distributor; Wichita-area dispensing sites)
records.free_claim: Charitable medication distributor delivering manufacturer-donated medicine free of cost to low-income, uninsured, chronically ill patients through a network of nonprofit dispensing sites (charitable pharmacies, FQHCs, safety-net clinics), including locations in Wichita, KS.
records.nonprofit_claim: Nonprofit charitable medication distributor; partner dispensing sites are not-for-profit organizations.
records.what: Nonprofit network supplying free chronic-condition medications to safety-net clinics/charitable pharmacies; patients access via partner dispensing sites in Kansas.

records.name: Medicare Extra Help / Part D Low-Income Subsidy (LIS) - Social Security Administration
records.free_claim: Federal program subsidizing Part D prescription drug costs (premiums, deductibles, copays) for low-income Medicare beneficiaries; in 2026 caps brand copays at $12.65 and generics at $5.10, with no copays after the catastrophic threshold. Apply free via SSA online, phone (1-800-772-1213), or local office.
records.nonprofit_claim: Federal government program administered by the Social Security Administration.
records.what: Government low-income subsidy that pays a large share of Medicare Part D drug costs for qualifying low-income beneficiaries nationwide, including Kansas.

records.name: NeedyMeds
records.free_claim: Free nonprofit information service helping patients find Patient Assistance Programs (PAPs) and other resources that provide low-cost and no-cost prescription medications; searchable by drug name or ZIP code. Helpline 1-800-503-6897.
records.nonprofit_claim: Nonprofit funded by donations and other sources.
records.what: National nonprofit directory connecting patients to manufacturer PAPs and free/low-cost medication programs.

records.name: RxAssist
records.free_claim: Free comprehensive directory of manufacturer Patient Assistance Programs helping patients obtain free or low-cost medications, plus tools for the uninsured.
records.nonprofit_claim: Nonprofit-operated patient assistance program resource (operated by Volunteers in Health Care / charitable program).
records.what: National directory of manufacturer PAPs and safety-net programs providing free/low-cost medications for uninsured patients.
